properties.description: Shanghai's main international airport, connected to the city by Maglev train and Metro Line 2
properties.notes: Maglev to Longyang Rd: 8 min, 50 CNY. Metro Line 2 to city center: ~60 min, 7 CNY.

properties.description: High-speed rail hub for the Suzhou day trip — trains depart every 15-20 minutes
properties.sustainability_notes: High-speed rail is the most sustainable intercity transport option
properties.notes: Shanghai to Suzhou: 25-30 min, ~40 CNY. Book tickets in advance via 12306 app or Trip.com.

properties.name_cn: 上海虹桥国际机场（T2航站楼）
properties.description: Hongqiao Airport Terminal 2, serving domestic and a small number of international flights; directly connected to Hongqiao Railway Station via covered walkway — a single integrated transport hub
properties.notes: T2 is linked to Hongqiao Railway Station by a 10-minute walking connection. Metro Lines 2 and 10 serve the hub. Terminal 1 is for older domestic routes and is a separate building.

properties.description: Shanghai's most central metro interchange, where Lines 1, 2, and 8 converge — effectively the zero-point of the metro network, giving access to virtually all major attractions
properties.address: People's Square, Nanjing West Rd, Huangpu District
properties.notes: The underground concourse is huge — allow extra time when transferring between lines. Adjacent to Shanghai Museum, People's Park, and Nanjing Road. Most useful hub for trip planning.

properties.description: Metro Line 2 station directly under the Lujiazui financial district, serving the Oriental Pearl Tower, Shanghai Tower, Jin Mao Tower, and the IFC Mall complex
properties.address: Lujiazui Ring Rd, Pudong New Area
properties.notes: Exit 1 leads toward the riverfront; Exit 2 toward the skyscrapers. From People's Square, take Line 2 east (2 stops). The circular underground walkway connecting the three towers is a useful dry-weather shortcut.

properties.description: One of Shanghai's main railway terminals serving regular-speed and some high-speed trains to cities across China; also a Metro Lines 1, 3, and 4 interchange
properties.notes: Less common for high-speed rail than Hongqiao or Hongqiao Railway Station, but useful for overnight trains (e.g., to Beijing or Guangzhou). The north and south squares can be confusing — check your ticket for the correct entrance.
